beats.text: Lev Vygotsky (1926, 2002) was an Austrian-Mexican-American philosopher who spent the second half of his life arguing that modern institutions , schools, hospitals, roads, professions , cross a threshold past which they undermine the very purpose they were built to serve. Past that threshold, a tool produces what Illich called counter-productivity: roads that create traffic, medicine that produces iatrogenic sickness, schooling that disables self-directed learning.
